Football.title: Antoine Semenyo joins forgotten Champions League winner in exclusive two-man club
Football.content:
Antoine Semenyo joined Steve Finnan in an exclusive club when making his Champions League debut against Real Madrid.

The January arrival made his first-ever European appearance on Wednesday night, as he was named in Manchester City's starting XI for their shock 3-0 defeat in the last 16 first leg at the Bernabeu.

4 Antoine Semenyo made his first Champions League start for Man City, after missing all of their league phase campaign Credit: Getty

It proved a baptism of fire for Semenyo, as City found themselves three down at half-time courtesy of a Federico Valverde hat-trick.



Despite this, it was still an historic night for the Ghana international, as he continued his meteoric rise up the football pyramid.

By featuring in the European tie, Semenyo became just the second player to play in all four divisions of English football, non-League and the Champions League.

Having started his career at Bristol City in the Championship, the winger earned his first stripes in men's football on loan at National League South club Bath City.

Further loan spells then followed at Newport County in League Two and with Sunderland, then in League One.



Semenyo then became a regular at Bristol City in the 2020/21 campaign, before a move to the Premier League with Bournemouth in January 2023.

Who else has achieved the feat?

The first player to achieve the same unique feat at Semenyo was former Liverpool defender Steve Finnan.



A Champions League winner with the Reds in 2005, Finnan began his senior career with non-league outfit Welling United.

He then climbed up the divisions, joining Birmingham City in the second tier in 1995, before a move to Notts County saw him feature in both third and fourth divisions.



Finnan made his Premier League debut with Fulham in 2001, where his performances saw him snapped up by Liverpool in 2003.

4 Steve Finnan made 217 appearances for Liverpool, after climbing from non-league to the Premier League

4 Semenyo joined Manchester City in January after scoring 10 goals for Bournemouth in the first half of the season Credit: Getty

His first Champions League appearance came in a qualifier against Grazer AK of Austria in the 2004/05 season, which ultimately saw the Reds triumph with the historic final victory over AC Milan in Istanbul.

Finnan started the final at right-back, but had to be substituted at half-time after picking up an injury, with Liverpool 3-0 down.



As history will tell you, Rafa Benitez's side famously fought back to make it 3-3, before clinching their fifth European glory on penalties, meaning Finnan picked up a winners' medal in his debut season.

City in need of a miracle

Semenyo will certainly be hoping he can continue to mirror the former Ireland international's career in European football.

Man City are now in need of a miracle of their own in the Champions League, after being left with a mountain to climb in the first leg of their Real Madrid clash.

Valverde put the Spanish giants ahead after 20 minutes when he pounced on a Gianluigi Donnarumma mistake to slot home from a narrow angle.

4 Federico Valverde stole the show in Semenyo's European debut by scoring a stunning 22-minute hat-trick in the first-half Credit: Getty



And the Uruguayan soon grabbed his second, with a clinical touch and finish into the far corner.

On the stroke of half-time, Valverde completed his first-ever career hat-trick with a stunning third goal that saw him flick the ball over Marc Guehi before volleying home.

This saw Semenyo endure a rare, disappointing night in his fledgling Man City career, following his £62.5million move from Bournemouth.

Since joining in January, the wide man has enjoyed an electric start to life at the Etihad, netting seven goals in 14 games.

He will hope to add to his tally against Real Madrid in the second leg next Tuesday, with coverage live on talkSPORT.



To tune in to talkSPORT or talkSPORT 2 through the website, click HERE for the live stream. Listen via the talkSPORT app, on DAB digital radio, through your smart speaker and on 1089 or 1053 AM.

Football.title: Petro Neto issues grovelling apology as Chelsea star avoids red card for shoving ball boy
Football.content:
Anything Eden Hazard can do, Pedro Neto can do better.

Well, maybe not skill-wise, but perhaps when it comes to dealing with ball kids.

4 Neto stirred the pot in Paris when he confronted a ball kid Credit: Getty

Neto sparked a brief fracas in the dying stages of Chelsea's 5-2 loss to Paris Saint-Germain when, in an attempt to speed up play, he tried to wrestle the ball off the child.

Net loss

Things quickly took a turn for the worse when Neto lightly shoved the youth, who crumpled to the ground.



Players from both teams ran in while medical staff attended to the child, who remained on the turf.

Eventually the dust settled and Neto escaped without so much as a formal warning from the referee for his conduct.

As for the ball kid, he eventually got back to his feet and the Portuguese was seen offering his apologies.

Neto reacts to ball kid incident

The Chelsea winger, who stressed the incident was out of character for him, even gave the ball kid his match-worn shirt at full-time.



"I want to say the situation that happened on the pitch with the ball boy, I want to apologise," Neto said.

"I already spoke with him. It was the heat of the moment. We were losing and I wanted to pick up the ball. I gave him a little push and that cannot happen.



"I gave him my shirt and I am really really sorry for that. In the heat of the moment, I wanted to get the ball fast.

"I said sorry about 35 times."

4 Enzo Fernandez went over to check if the ball kid was ok Credit: Getty



Chelsea boss Liam Rosenior also addressed Neto's flashpoint, although he was more concerned by the fact his side conceded shortly after the scuffle rather than the incident itself.

"I haven't seen what happened," Rosenior said.

"I heard there was an altercation with a ball boy and you never want to see that. I've been told Pedro has apologised. I have to see it back and speak on it in the days to come.

"In that moment, as a group - that's happened. Switch on. We didn't and we were punished by a good team."

Hazard sign

Neto's brief conflict with the ball kid gave Chelsea fans flashbacks of Hazard's infamous confrontation during the Blues' League Cup semi-final against Swansea City in 2013.

Hazard tried to speed proceedings up at the Liberty Stadium, only for ball kid Charlie Morgan to lie on the ball.



4 Hazard earned himself an early shower when he kicked a ball boy Credit: Getty

The Belgian whiz then proceeded to kick Morgan, who wasn't going to give up the ball without a fight.

Hazard was sent off for his actions and later charged by the FA with violent conduct.

Luckily that was as far as the incident went as Morgan, who later went on to found a £150million vodka business, elected not to press charges.

Parisian Blues

Neto's brouhaha with the ball kid was just one of many low-lights for Chelsea in the French capital on Wednesday.



Blues boss Rosenior rolled the dice and started Filip Jorgensen between the sticks ahead of Robert Sanchez.

It would be Jorgensen's third start for Chelsea in the Champions League this season, but it became a night to forget for the Dane as the visitors shipped an alarming five goals.

4 Jorgensen struggled to make the most of a rare start for Chelsea Credit: AFP

PSG's third goal will prove especially haunting for Jorgensen, as his careless pass from the back was intercepted by Bradley Barcola on the edge of Chelsea's 18-yard box.

The ball eventually fell to Vitinha, who deftly lobbed a desperate Jorgensen in the 74th minute.



From there, it was one-way traffic for PSG as a late double from Khvicha Kvaratskhelia left Chelsea needing to overcome a three-goal deficit in the second leg, which will take place at Stamford Bridge on Tuesday.

Football.title: Jude Bellingham has priceless reaction to Federico Valverde’s ‘Puskas contender’ as Real Madrid star matches Lionel Messi feat
Football.content:
Jude Bellingham has priceless reaction to Federico Valverde’s ‘Puskas contender’ as Real Madrid star matches Lionel Messi feat

Federico Valverde may have won himself the Puskas Award against Manchester City with a remarkable goal on Wednesday night.

The Real Madrid midfielder emulated Lionel Messi at the exact same time, scoring a first-half hat-trick in the Champions League.

3 Valverde scored a stunning 22-minute hat-trick in the first half against Man City Credit: Getty

Man City's start to the first leg of their last 16 tie in Madrid couldn't have started much worse, with Valverde putting them to the world spectacularly.



He scored his opening goal after 20 minutes, rounding City goalkeeper Gianluigi Donnarumma to tap into an empty net.

Then, just seven minutes later, he superbly fired into the bottom corner past Donnarumma again to make it 2-0.

And Valverde saved the best until last, netting an incredible third goal in just 22 minutes to score his first career hat-trick.

The 27-year-old raced into the box and received a neat chipped pass from Brahim Diaz, meeting it with a perfect touch.



His first touch lifted the ball perfectly over Marc Guehi's head, before he ran onto it again and volleyed home before it touched the ground.

On the sumptuous hat-trick goal, talkSPORT's Scott Minto said on commentary: "That is a world-class goal from a world-class player.



"Anyone who gets the chance to see this goal at any point in the future, please do... he's running at pace, the first touch is perfect, over Guehi and enough for him to volley it in.

"It's just an unbelievable goal... it's three moments of genius from the same player."



Host Adrian Durham then later added: "It's a Puskas contender."

3 Valverde's third goal of the game was labelled as a 'Puskas contender' Credit: Getty

Valverde's treble was all the match had to offer in terms of goals with the Bernabeu clash finishing 3-0, although the home side missed a glorious chance to get their fourth in the second half when Vinicius Jr had a penalty saved by Gianluigi Donnarumma.

On top of his potentially Puskas-winning strike, Valverde also joined a footballing icon with his first-half hat-trick.

He is just the second player to score a first-half hat-trick against an English opponent in the Champions League after Messi did so for Barcelona against Arsenal in April 2010.

Bellingham can't believe it

Not only were talkSPORT's commentary team in awe of Valverde's third goal, but his injured teammate Jude Bellingham was too.



Bellingham, who is sidelined due to a hamstring injury picked up not long after undergoing shoulder surgery, was left with his mouth wide open as he watched a replay.

His eyes widened and he was left in disbelief as he clapped into the air whilst watching on before making a praise gesture.

3 Bellingham was left in complete awe of his teammate's incredible goal

Man City slammed

After Valverde's hat-trick, Durham then went on a half-time rant about Man City's performance that saw them go 3-0 down.

He brutally summarised on-air: "Marc Guehi, ordinary, he just stands out of the way for that third goal.



"Antoine Semenyo, out of his depth, has offered nothing.

"O'Reilly's doing a Kinsky, Doku and Savinho, we highlighted it before, they are no threat whatsoever...Man City have been truly terrible."

Pep Guardiola's side went on to lose the first leg 3-0 and they have a mountain to climb in the second leg back in Manchester, which will be live on talkSPORT next Tuesday.

News.title: Iran’s impotent new Ayatollah is in a COMA and has lost at least one leg – as his rogue regime plunges world into chaos
News.content:
IRAN is waging a war detonating global chaos led by a man in a coma, it was revealed today.

New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has been put in charge by the rogue state’s mad mullahs despite being critically wounded in an air strike.





Sources told The Sun the 56-year-old son of assassinated Ayatollah Ali Khamenei lost at least one leg and has also suffered serious stomach or liver damage.

It was unclear whether he was injured on the same day his 86-year-old father died – February 28 – but he is not directing the strategy currently sparking energy market turmoil.

A Sun source in Iranian capital Tehran today smuggled out extraordinary detail about the new leader’s condition, treatment, location and security.



He is currently under intensive care at the Sina University Hospital in the city’s historic quarter after a section of the building was sealed off surrounded by massive security.

The source – who asked not to be identified fearing for his life – managed to dodge Iran’s near total internet blackout to send messages to an exiled dissident based in London.

He is not a medic but knows members of the hospital’s trauma team who told him Mojtaba was in “very serious” condition under the care of Mohammad Reza Zafargjani.

Zafargjani is Iran’s Minister of health, Treatment and Medical Education but also one of the country’s top trauma surgeons.



The Sun source said in his message: “One or two of his legs have been cut off. His liver or stomach has also ruptured. He is apparently in a coma as well.”

Zafargjani’s Persian Wikipedia page reveals he honed his skills after eight years operating in the bloody Iran-Iraq War in the 1980’s.

The 70-year-old surgeon was himself wounded by chemical weapons in the Iran Iraq war and is the Islamist regime’s most trusted physician.

Our source’s detailed revelations are impossible to verify under Iran’s current internet blackout but Mojtaba status among Iran’s thousands of war wounded has already been confirmed.

Iranian state TV now openly refers to the late ayatollah’s silent successor as “Jaanbaz of Ramadan” – wounded war veteran.



And the rogue nation’s continuing attacks on neighbouring states – which have sent oil and gas prices soaring – supports theories that Iran is now being run by a “Ghost Ayatollah.”

Fighting has continued despite the loss of two ayatollahs in a matter of days after Khamenei set up 31 regional commands of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps to take over if the regime was decapitated.

And commanders spread across the nation are now under orders to fight on indefinitely – with no leader in place to call a halt.

Iranian social media sources added further detail as Mojtaba’s critical condition was laid bare for the first time today.

Veteran surgeon Zafargjani was said to have been assisted by Dr. Mohammad Marashi, a senior surgeon at Shahid Beheshti University of Medical Sciences.



Marashi is the brother of former President Akbar Hashemi Rafsanjani’s wife and another trusted figure close to the heart of the hardline regime.

Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian was also reported to have visited the Sina Hospital two days ago accompanied by Dr. Zafarghandi.

And speculation followed that Pezeshkian was secretly visiting Mojtaba and is being kept fully briefed on his condition.

Security at the hospital was said to be extremely tight, with access to the intensive care unit restricted to only a handful of authorized individuals.

Other sources reported alleged Mojtada is entirely unaware of the ongoing war, the deaths of family members including his wife and son and even his own election as Supreme Leader.



Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei put his nation’s war machine on autopilot before he was wiped out in the first action of Operation Epic Fury.

His shadowy son Mojtaba, 56, succeeded him five days ago but has never given a public speech and has stayed silent amid rumours he is already dead.

And Iranian insiders told The Sun this was his evil father’s grand plan – and that troops launching drones and missiles have been primed to fight on, led by “a ghost.”

The fate of Mojtaba – who lost his wife, Zahra, eight-year-old son Bagher as well as his mother and sister in the blast which killed his 86-year-old father – remained unclear last night.

But State TV is now openly referring to the late ayatollah’s silent successor as “Jaanbaz of Ramadan” – wounded war veteran.



Evil Khamenei’s Snr’s legacy will be spiralling World War III fears coupled with higher bills and economic misery for Brits and consumers across the globe.

Who is the new Ayatollah? Mojtaba Khamenei, 56, the son of the slain Ayatollah Ali Khamenei , was chosen as the regime’s next dictator Mojtaba was selected as the primary candidate to succeed his own dad following two Assembly of Experts meetings in Iran. He is understood to be a hardline conservative cleric who advocated for developing nuclear bombs- and who has strong links to Iran’s brutal Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC). Mojtaba owns luxury pads in the UK worth more than £100million, including 11 homes in Bishop’s Avenue – nicknamed Billionaire’s Row – according to Bloomberg. He is believed to control a sprawling investment empire across Tehran, Dubai and Frankfurt – and has reportedly stashed cash away in Swiss bank accounts. Before his dad’s assassination, he taught at the country’s largest seminary in Qom – having been previously educated by an extreme cleric known as the “Crocodile Ayatollah”. Mojtaba, who reportedly has “a bleak human rights record”, has helped suppress domestic protests and also served in the Iran–Iraq War. He was previously predicted as next Supreme Leader – with a Middle East Institute report in 2022 saying “there is no doubt that Mojtaba wants to be supreme leader”.

Before he was blown to bits in his presidential palace, the tyrant had spent years honing a strategy to kick into gear in the event of his assassination.

Khamenei watched and learned in 2003 when US forces conquered neighbour Iraq in 2003 by capturing and killing leader Saddam Hussein and his top lieutenants.

He appointed trusted henchman Major General Mohammad Ali Jafari who spent the next four years devising a contingency plan.

Jafari divided Iran – which is seven times bigger than the UK – into 31 autonomous command centres of the all-powerful Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps.



Iranian sources and analysts revealed yesterday that these self-governing commands are now running Iran’s war and suppressing dissent without directions from a leader in capital Tehran.

An exiled Iranian analyst – who asked not to be identified for fear of reprisals – told the Sun: “Mojtaba Khamenei has not spoken since his father was killed – he has not appeared at all on TV.

“He has not issued any kind of statement or said a single word.

“There has been no stirring address to the armed forces he now commands and that has fuelled speculation that he is seriously wounded or even dead.

“But that doesn’t matter any more.



“His father left behind a system which has kicked into gear which can be run by a ghost.

“That is why Iran’s missiles and drones are still flying and it’s why the war will go on.”

Mojtaba may be in the shadows but he is known to have close ties with Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard and its brutal protest-crushing paramilitary arm, known as The Basij.

And experts say fanatical troops will remain loyal to him, even if he is little more than a silent figurehead.

Mojtaba reportedly controls a £70million property portfolio in London and reportedly needed four hospital visits in the UK capital to be treated for impotency, according to US intelligence.



Even before the war, Iran’s new Supreme Leader was known to be more extreme than his hate-filled father.

But Mojtaba is now potentially even more dangerous after his loathing for his western enemies was fuelled by the loss of his close family.

And his heavily-armed and motivated henchmen will share his lust for revenge – whether he is dead or alive.

An Israeli government source told The Sun today/yesterday: “It is absolutely irrefutable that Mojtaba Khamenei has been wounded.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“But his condition actually has little bearing on the course of the war as he is a puppet put in place by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.



“They hold the power now and they are running Iran.”

News.title: Trans killer Robert Dorgan’s son scores overtime winning goal in key ice hockey game – & dedicates it to murdered family
News.content:
THE son of a transgender killer who gunned down an ex-wife, son and grandad has scored a last-minute goal in a key ice hockey match – and dedicated it to his slain loved ones.

Star defenseman Colin Dorgan netted a vital game-winner in the dying moments of double overtime – securing his team a place in the championship.





It comes after his father Robert Dorgan, 56, opened fire at an ice hockey game in Rhode Island on February 16 – killing three family members and also dying from self-inflicted gunshot wounds.

In a heart-warming tribute to his late mum Rhonda, 52, brother Aidan, 23, and grandad Gerald, 75, Colin scored a double OT winner on Wednesday night.

His Beavers teammates mobbed him on the ice as they celebrated what Colin described as “the greatest moment of my life”.

The composed last-minute finish against Portsmouth in the final 48 seconds of the game sent his high school team to the Division II final.



Blackstone Valley Schools’ star captain said his mum, brother and grandad earned the assist as he wore a jersey with their initials on it during the match.

The school’s head coach Chris Librizzi said: “I literally cried like a baby, the floodgates opened.

“When I saw him open, I knew he was golden.”

Colin said: “My good buddy fed me the puck and I could hear the crowd.



“It was a surreal moment.”

The senior also told 12 News that his teammates have always been there for him – especially in the wake of the deadly shooting at Lynch Arena last month.

“We have to stick together,” Colin said.

“It’s a hard time for everyone. We have each other’s back and we truly love each other… These are my brothers.”

Librizzi also revealed how the entire hockey team rallied around Colin and his sister Ava following the tragedy.



“We got together every single day,” Librizzi said.

“We skated, worked out, or had dinner at night together for 14 days straight. I believe that made a difference.”

It comes after reports emerged that Rhonda, who had married the attacker in 1992, was the killer’s first cousin once removed.



Robert had also recently undergone gender reassignment surgery before the horror shooting.

Court documents show Rhonda filed for separation in 2020, writing “gender reassignment surgery” and “narcissistic personality disorder” as grounds for divorce.

A twisted image posted online shows a smiling Robert inked with a large SS symbol.

Several racist rants from the alleged killer have also been uncovered.

Robert praised Adolf Hitler, spoke out in support of “white power”, and spouted antisemitic views along with slurs, social media accounts linked to the suspect show.



It comes after Dorgan’s daughter Amanda Wallace-Hubbard, 36, revealed that she thought she and her sons were going to be killed next in the horror attack.

She told the New York Post: “I turned around and Rob was looking into my eyes, and I really felt like we were next.”

Relatives say the killer was on a mission to wipe out the entire family – but the rampage ended when a heroic bystander and Aidan bravely tried to wrestle the gun away.



Describing the moment their eyes met, Amanda said: “It feels very odd to say this, but I felt like I saw him communicating deep sadness.

“I witnessed him shooting my brother, and then I ducked for cover on top of my sons.

“I was really in fear for my son’s lives. At the time when the good Samaritans stepped in and really incapacitated him, we were the only members of the family who had not been shot.”

The shooter’s daughter explained that the shooting was “one person’s vendetta against their family”.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Amanda told WPRI: “We were targeted. This was very specific. It wasn’t random.“



She added that her killer dad “never really saw the error in his ways” and refused to get help for mental health issues.

News.title: Ominous texts nurse ‘sent married co-worker mom, 35, he was having affair with – before luring her to her brutal death’
News.content:
DEADLY 'TRYST' Ominous texts nurse ‘sent married co-worker mom, 35, he was having affair with – before luring her to her brutal death’

A NURSE allegedly beat his married ex-colleague to death after luring her with texts promising a romantic birthday celebration.

Rene Perez, 38, was arrested a year and a half after a Linda Campitelli was killed.



Campitelli died after sustaining skull and rib fractures in October 2024.



Perez was arrested on Tuesday in Miami, Florida, said Palm Beach County Sheriff’s Office.

He was charged with first-degree murder, possession of a deadly weapon and tampering with evidence.

Campitelli and Perez were both married and were having an affair for around two years after meeting at Wellington Regional Medical Center, where they both worked, reports The New York Post.

Nurse told affair partner he wanted to be “romantic”

They met on October 28, 2024, to celebrate Campitelli’s birthday. She told her husband that she was meeting her friends, according to reports.

Court papers show that Campitelli texted Perez on WhatsApp to say she was nervous about the meetup.

“I LOVE YOU, I FEEL KINDA WEIRD. I DON’T KNOW WHAT TO EXPECT TOMORROW. YOU’VE NEVER DONE ANYTHING LIKE THIS FOR ME BEFORE AND I FEEL A LITTLE NERVOUS,” Campitelli wrote.

“LOL, IT’S NO BIG DEAL. JUST TRYING TO SHOW U THAT I CAN BE ROMANTIC. IT PROBABLY WONT BE AS GOOD AS WHAT YOUVE DONE FOR ME,” Perez replied.



Her car arrived at the Retina Group of Florida Building in Wellington, where Perez previously worked, said police.

Perez had put down a blanket that said “HAPPY BIRTHDAY HOPE YOUR BIRTHDAY IS OUT OF THIS WORLD”.

He also had medical sheets used to control odor and moisture in the back of the car, which had its back seats laid down flat, court records show.

Campitelli’s body was found at 10:20pm 50 feet from her car.

She had suffered blunt force trauma to her head and torso, four lacerations to her scalp and a fatal build-up of blood in her skull.



Campitelli appeared to have been dragged from her car, as indicated by scuff marks and road rash.

There were blood stains on her Chevy Tahoe’s rear driver’s side door handle and her Apple Watch was found inside soaked in blood.

Perez was seen on CCTV walking towards Delray Medical Center, his workplace, and throwing something in the trash.



He told police he canceled the celebration because his son was sick but the text messages appeared to refute this.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Perez was arrested after witness interviews, 50 search warrants, and cell phone data analysis, reports WPTV.

He is being held without bond.

News.title: Married woman, 38, in court over ‘sexual assault’ of teen tourist in Magaluf hotel steam room after she ‘got horny’
News.content:
A MARRIED woman charged with groping a teen boy on holiday after she “got horny” will face trial as she “wants her day in court” to clear her name.

The 38-year-old unnamed Irish woman allegedly assaulted the 18-year-old while staying at the swanky Martinique Hotel with her husband in Magaluf.





A preliminary hearing in the Majorcan capital Palma to see if a plea bargain deal could be thrashed out ended in stalemate.

Her lawyer, Joan Arbos, said his client would insist she committed no crime despite her arrest for touching the Swedish man’s genitals without his consent in a Turkish steam bath.

Well-placed sources at the hotel claim the woman, then aged 37, struck up a conversation with the young Swedish man in the hotel sauna on the evening of June 3.

Her accuser alleges she then followed him into the hotel’s Turkish steam bath and, when no-one else was around, jumped in “with a lustful spirit” before fondling his genitals without consent.

The assault ended with her accuser saying “no, no, no” and leaving.

After alerting hotel staff, the woman was swiftly apprehended and whisked away to the police station.

Reports at the time said the Irish woman, who was born in Brazil, was with her husband at the time of her arrest.



According to CrimeWorld, she told the court the next day: “I got horny. I thought he was interested in me too, that we were flirting,

“I apologised right away. I was very embarrassed, I’m sorry.”

She spent a night in a police cell last summer before being released on bail and was free to travel back home to County Cork, Ireland pending an ongoing criminal probe.

Public prosecutors have demanded a one-and-a-half year jail term on conviction in a three-page indictment.

As well as the prison term, prosecutors want the Irish tourist banned from working with minors for two years if convicted.



They also want her to pay her male accuser £440 in compensation for the trauma he allegedly suffered.

Two police officers will be called to testify along with the alleged offender and victim.

But Mr Arbos said his client “does not accept she committed a crime” and the incident “was a simple misunderstanding.”

A source close to the case added: “She didn’t deny touching this man’s privates in her first court appearance.

“She explained she acted the way she did because she thought he was interested in her.



“She confirmed she took her hand away as soon as he protested and she realised she’d read the situation wrong.”

The woman will attend court to “clear her name” even though she is not obliged to be present, the source said.

She initially denied touching the man’s genitals to police before admitting to a “misunderstanding.”

Mr Arbos said shortly after she was arrested he was going to ask for the case to be “discontinued” against his client on the basis he “didn’t consider a crime had taken place.”

A spokesman for the Civil Guard said after the Magaluf hotel incident: “The alleged sexual aggressor, who was in a sauna with a younger Swedish man, was accused of touching the victim’s private parts without consent.



“When officers arrived the victim was visibly upset.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“After carrying out inquiries, they proceeded to arrest the suspected offender, who was handed over to the courts.”

Prison sentences of two years or less are normally suspended in Spain for first-time offenders.

News.title: Should PM be sacked for poor judgment over Mandy scandal? VOTE NOW
News.content:
POLL OF THE DAY

POLL OF THE DAY Should PM be sacked for poor judgment over Mandy scandal? VOTE NOW

SIR Keir Starmer is under mounting pressure after files revealed he knew Peter Mandelson was a “reputational risk” before appointing him as UK ambassador to the US.

The dossier shows the PM was warned about the Labour peer’s links to disgraced financier and paedophile Jeffrey Epstein.





Yet despite the warnings, the PM pressed ahead with the decision to send Peter Mandelson to Washington.

The files also indicate Sir Keir had been told Mandelson continued his relationship with Epstein even after the financier’s 2008 conviction.

But Mandelson was eventually sacked just months after taking the post.



The controversy deepened when Mandelson demanded a staggering £547,000 pay-off following his departure.

Instead, he walked away with £75,000 of taxpayer-funded cash as well as a discretionary payment worth more than £34,000.

Officials said Mandelson was legally entitled to three months of his £157,000 salary, but critics say the extra payment raises serious questions.

Tory shadow minister Neil O’Brien blasted the deal as “hush money”, claiming it was intended to keep Mandelson quiet about the affair.



Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch accused the PM of misleading the country, saying the files show he had not been honest with the public or Parliament about what he knew.

And Reform UK chairman Zia Yusuf piled on the pressure.

He Starmer was warned about Mandelson’s friendship with Epstein but appointed him anyway.

The Reform UK chairman added: “Starmer lied. Starmer must resign.”

Have your say in our exclusive poll.



Love to debate? Join Sun Club for just £1.99 a month for the boldest opinion on the issues shaping Britain.

News.title: Incredible pics show Sahara Desert blanketed by SNOW as plunging temperatures hit desert & turn iconic sand dunes white
News.content:
STUNNING photos show the iconic Sahara Desert draped in snow as temperatures drop to record lows.

The world famous burnt orange sand dunes have been turned white as temperatures drop below freezing in Algeria.





The low temperatures have created the perfect conditions for snowfall, surprising both tourists and locals.

It is believed to be the seventh time in the last four decades that snow has fallen in this region of the desert.

Prior to 2016, the last time snow fell in the town of Aïn Séfra was in 1979.

The town – whose name literally translates to “yellow spring” – is known as the “Gateway to the Desert.”

Located on the northerly edge of the Sahara, close to the foothills of the Atlas Mountains, it provided the perfect weather conditions for the pretty phenomenon.

While it was an exciting moment for residents and travellers, the powder shower only lasted fleeting moments, with the snow melting almost as quickly as it fell.

The last time snow fell in the region was in January last year.



In 2021, the eastern side of the desert was blanketed in snow, which extended across the Red Sea to Saudi Arabia.

The shock weather conditions saw the mercury hit -2C.

The area – which is close to the border with Jordan – experienced unusual weather.

The desert kingdom can experience highs of more than 50C during the hot season.

At the time, snow also fell near the desert town of Aïn Séfra in Algeria.



Sheep were seen standing on the ice-covered dunes as temperatures dipped to -3C.

Saudi Arabia also experienced snow in 2018 and locals had fun with sleighs and snowballs.

Snow also fell in Lebanon, Syria and Iran – where some regions were buried under 4ft.

News.title: Fury as council put up sign threatening anyone feeding birds with FIVE YEARS in prison
News.content:
COO BLIMEY Fury as council put up sign threatening anyone feeding birds with FIVE YEARS in prison

A COUNCIL has put up a sign threatening anyone feeding birds with five years in prison sparking fury among residents.

The menacing notice from Brent Council, London, also threatens an unlimited fine for “crimes against our streets”.





Translated into a number of South Asian languages and branded with the council logo, it can be found on Christchurch Avenue in Kilburn.

While it also states the reward for not feeding the birds is “cleaner streets”.

Brent Council currently has a Public Space Protection Order (PSPO) in place which bans the feeding of wild animals in parks, open spaces and cemeteries.

But five years behind bars is usually reserved for violent crimes such as causing grievous bodily harm, firearm offences and supplying drugs.

Michael Britton, a wildlife rescue volunteer, told the Local Democracy Service the sign was “outrageous” and “completely unacceptable and morally wrong”.

“I firmly believe that threatening five years in prison or an unlimited fine is both disgraceful, shockingly heavy handed and clearly illegal,” he said.

“I personally find the signs bullying, dictatorial, threatening, and racist; targeting and scapegoating specific cultures for ‘committing crimes against our streets’.”

Britton called for a review of the council’s PSPO strategy and said it “someone must be held accountable”.



“If we allow the council to diminish what little compassion remains in our society, we’re all doomed,” he added.

Nearly two weeks ago, a London student took to Reddit to say they had been slapped by a £100 fine by Brent Council for feeding bread to pigeons in Kilburn Park.

“London finally had some sun today. So I went out for a walk to the park. Just to enjoy the weather and clear my head. I had a slice of bread with me and I fed it to some pigeons along the way,” she said.

“Next thing I know, two enforcement officers are walking up to me and handing me a fine for £100.

“They pointed to a small signboard with only small icons marked in red, fixed 7ft up a distant pole- I’m 5ft tall with autism, I can barely make eye contact with the world… how was I supposed to make out that sign?”



She added that £100 was a “month’s groceries for me”.

Brent Council confirmed that the sign is now being removed.

While Cllr Krupa Sheth, Brent Council Cabinet Member for Public Realm and Enforcement, said: “We are sorry for any confusion caused by this old sign. While Brent takes a tough approach to environmental offences, the wording does not reflect how we would communicate with residents today.

“What has not changed is our determination to keep our neighbourhoods clean and safe.

“Our enforcement work is making a real difference: fly tipping incidents in Brent have fallen by more than half, down 53% from 34,830 to 16,338 between, while fly tipping fines have increased more than fivefold, the second highest number in England.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

“That reflects a zero-tolerance approach to environmental crime, backed by more enforcement officers, stronger investigations and better use of technology.

“We will always be firm where behaviour harms our communities, but we also want our messaging to be clear, proportionate and fair.”

News.title: UAE steps up crackdown on residents filming Iran blitz as 21 charged including Dubai Brit who ‘deleted vid IMMEDIATELY’
News.content:
A BRITISH tourist is among 21 people charged under cyber-crime laws in the United Arab Emirates after allegedly filming Iranian missiles causing carnage in Dubai.

The 60-year-old was arrested on Monday – despite assuring police that he deleted the footage from his phone.





Regardless, the Londoner was caught up in the mass crackdown, campaign group Detained in Dubai warned.

Radha Stirling, CEO of Detained in Dubai, described the list of charges as “extremely vague but serious on paper”.

“In reality, the alleged conduct could be something as simple as sharing or commenting on a video that is already circulating online,” she said.



“Under UAE cyber-crime laws, the person who originally posts content can be charged, but so can anyone who reshapes, reposts or comments on it.

“One video can quickly lead to dozens of people facing criminal charges.”

All 21 people are accused of using an information network or information technology tool to broadcast, publish, republish or circulate false news, rumours or provocative propaganda that may incite public opinion or disturb public security.

It comes as…



If found guilty, they could face up to two years in prison, or fines ranging from £4,000 to £40,600, or both.

Foreign nationals will also be hit with enforced deportations.

Stirling also warned that multiple counts can be applied.

This means that a person who reposts several videos or articles could face cumulative charges and multiple sentences.

“There are countless images, videos and news reports circulating online about the conflict,” the CEO explained.



“People understandably assume that if something is already widely shared or published by media outlets, it must be acceptable to comment on or repost it.

“In the UAE, that assumption can be extremely dangerous.”

Raphaella Stirling, a crisis manager at the organisation, confirmed they are continuing to monitor the cases and working to establish the full details of the allegations and the nationalities of the other individuals charged.

“The family is extremely worried,” she said.

“It’s a natural reflex to photograph your surroundings but authorities are hypersensitive and the real danger may not be Iran‘s missiles overhead but the Emirate authorities’ react to what people post online.”



The UAE's cryptic cyber-crime laws The UAE’s cyber-crime laws are among the strictest in the world. People can face fines of up to £57,000, lengthy detention, and travel bans for social media posts that authorities consider harmful to “public order”, “national unity” or the reputation of the state. Detained in Dubai warns that the legislation is “deliberately broad” – and in times of war they become even more dangerous. Almost anything critical of government policy or regional conflict can be interpreted as a crime. In practice, commentary about regional conflict, government policy or security matters can be interpreted as a criminal offence. Sharing advice about what to do and what not to do in the country, the organisation said: “Do not assume your home country’s free speech protections apply in the UAE. They do not. “Do not assume that sharing international news coverage is safe. It may not be. “Do not assume that deleting a post removes liability. It often does not.”

This comes just two weeks after the UAE Public Prosecution Office warned against publishing or circulating “rumours” and information from unknown sources.

A statement said: “The Public Prosecution warns against publishing or circulating rumours and information from unknown sources through social media platforms or any other technological means, due to the consequences thereof, including the spread of confusion and harm to the security and stability of society.

“The Public Prosecution further emphasises the necessity of obtaining information solely from official and accredited sources, and refraining from being influenced by content that is published or circulated without verification.

“Any person who shares or reposts content from unknown sources shall be subject to legal accountability in accordance with the applicable legislation, even if they are not the original creator of such content.”



Elsewhere in the Gulf, more than 200 people in Qatar have already been charged under similar laws since the conflict began, according to Detained in Dubai.

For years, Dubai has cultivated a reputation as a glamorous and attractive destination – and more than 250,000 British nationals have made it their home in recent years.

But many have fled amid the onslaught of Iranian drones and rockets in the past two weeks.

Over the weekend, Iran’s President Masoud Pezeshkian publicly apologised to neighbours, saying in a recorded video message that strikes would stop unless strikes on Iran originated from the Gulf region.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

But soon after, plumes of smoke rose above Dubai International Airport as an Iranian drone smashed near a terminal.

The Sun has contacted the UK Foreign Office and the UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs for comment.

News.title: Next's 'perfect' Mother's Day gift set hamper that's usually £169 now only £25
News.content:
Next's 'perfect' Mother's Day gift set hamper that's usually £169 now only £25

Shoppers looking to spoil their mum this Mother's Day can check out Next's £25 beauty box

View 3 Images Next says the box is filled with expertly handpicked premium products (Image: Getty)

Next is selling a beauty hamper full of six high-end products that could be the 'perfect' Mother's Day present for anyone keen to 'spoil' a special mum in their lives. The store's Mother's Day Spoil Her Beauty Box is worth £169, but is on sale for only £25.

‌



Inside, shoppers can choose from a mix of full-size and travel-size items, including a couple of deluxe sample options. By opting for Next's Mother's Day Spoil Her Beauty Box, customers can save £144, instead of purchasing each item separately.

‌



To add that personalised touch, shoppers can choose between 'All About Her' and 'All About Mum' messaging on the packaging. The box contains a hand cream, eye treatment, face mask, shower foam and more from 'luxury' brands like Rituals, L'Occitane and Neal's Yard Remedies.

It continues: "Surprise and delight her with skincare from Caudalie and BeautyPro, along with body care from L'Oréal Paris and Murad, to name just a few, for a spa day at home and a gift she will treasure."

View 3 Images The Mother's Day Spoil Her Beauty Box is worth £169 (Image: Next)

Please consult your doctor about beauty products and skincare ingredients to avoid whilst pregnant, as some ingredients may not be suitable during pregnancy or breastfeeding."

Article continues below

Elsewhere, there are plenty of other beauty boxes on the market. Home Bargains is selling its Made Up Beauty Box, a 15-piece beauty set full of products worth £210, for only £29.99. The curated beauty box features skincare, haircare and self-care products and includes a mix of full-size and deluxe-size items. It comes in a "stylish" lilac presentation box, making it "ideal for gifting or personal pampering".

At M&S, shoppers can get the Unwind Edit, packed with brands like NEOM, Estée Lauder, Emma Hardie, and more, for £30. The collection includes essentials such as a silk eye mask, a pillow sleep mist, and various overnight treatments.

News.title: Anyone with a garden urged to drill holes in their fences before spring arrives
News.content:
Anyone with a garden urged to drill holes in their fences before spring arrives

Wildlife experts are urging gardeners to drill CD-case-sized holes in their fences before spring to help endangered wildlife roam safely between gardens as they wake from hibernation

View 2 Images Anyone with a garden has been urged to make holes in their fences before spring arrives (Image: Getty)

Wildlife experts are encouraging green-fingered Brits to drill holes in the base of their fences ahead of the spring season, in a bid to aid one of our most cherished - and most threatened - native species.

‌



As hedgehogs begin to stir from their winter slumber, they could do with a helping hand in our gardens. So, if you're keen on making your patch more hedgehog-friendly, it might be time to inspect your fence.

‌



Drilling holes in your fence might not top your list of favourite gardening chores, but according to wildlife specialists, it's a move that could significantly benefit our prickly pals. Known as a hedgehog highway, these gaps offer a safe passage for hedgehogs to find food and shelter, away from harm's way.

‌



View 2 Images Gardeners are being urged to make holes in fences to help hedgehogs (Image: Getty)

Hedgehog highways are essentially a network of gaps and holes cut through hedges and fences, creating secure corridors for hedgehogs to navigate, reports the Express.

Given the risk posed by roads, these highways offer hedgehogs a safe alternative for their travels. Anton Baskerville, a wildlife expert at Woodlands.co.uk, shed some light on the matter: "Hedgehogs like to roam large distances to find food, mates, and nesting sites.

‌



"In urban environments, fences, walls, and other barriers can limit their movement, making it harder for them to meet these basic needs. A hedgehog highway enables them to safely travel between gardens and other habitats, greatly improving their chances of survival and reproduction."

Across Britain, hedgehogs are officially classified as being under threat of extinction. Numbers have plummeted dramatically, especially in rural areas, due to habitat loss, road casualties, and dwindling food supplies.

The brilliant news about creating a hedgehog highway is that you needn't tear down a massive chunk of your hedge or fencing.

‌



Thomson Environmental Consultants recommends: "Creating a CD-case-sized gap in garden fences allows hedgehogs to move freely between gardens.

"By coordinating with your neighbours, you can establish a connected network of gardens and mark gaps to raise awareness."

In a clip posted on TikTok, gardening expert Deb revealed some straightforward advice for attracting them. She explained there are effortless methods to lure them in. To encourage their visits, she mentioned you simply need to implement one key strategy.

Article continues below

"A log pile, compost heap or purpose-built house in a shaded spot will give hedgehogs a safe place to rest and hibernate. Provide food and water, not milk. Contrary to popular belief, milk is bad for hedgehogs. Do not leave milk out. Leave out fresh water."

She mentioned you can also purchase hedgehog biscuits to scatter around your garden for them. This will offer them some delicious treats to enjoy.

She also mentioned that hedgehogs are fond of gardens with leafy trees, so it's worth bearing this in mind. If you don't have these, Deb suggested it might be a good idea to plant some.

News.title: Reform accused of fighting like 'rats in sack' over charging for the NHS
News.content:
Reform accused of fighting like 'rats in sack' over charging for the NHS

Welsh leader Dan Thomas went against Nigel Farage by ruling out changing the NHS to an insurance-based healthcare system, despite the Reform UK leader failing to do so just one week earlier

View 2 Images Nigel Farage has refused to rule out an insurance-based model for the NHS (Image: PA)

Reform has been accused of “fighting like rats in a sack” after a row emerged over charging Brits for healthcare.

‌



Welsh leader Dan Thomas went against Nigel Farage by ruling out changing the NHS to an insurance-based healthcare system, despite the Reform UK leader failing to do so just one week earlier.

‌



Mr Farage had told journalists “that would be a national decision ahead of a general election". He has previously flirted with the idea - but has issued conflicting messages, including the NHS remaining free at the point of use. Mr Thomas has now promised no privatisation.

‌



Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

Flip-flopping Nigel Farage shifts Iran war stance as fears mount over bill hikes READ MORE :

View 2 Images Reform UK leader in Wales Dan Thomas disagrees with the Reform UK leader (Image: PA)

‌



Asked if he disagreed with Farage’s remarks on an insurance model, Mr Thomas replied: “Look, Nigel’s also said that devolved issues are down to the Welsh party, and I wouldn’t consider any kind of insurance-based or private-based system for the Welsh NHS.”

Responding, Stephen Kinnock MP, Health Minister said: "Reform are now fighting like rats in a sack over whether or not to charge you for healthcare. Nigel Farage has a long history of pushing for an insurance-based system of healthcare - and we know when his right-hand men fall out of line, he's more than willing to throw them under the bus to get his own way.

"Destroying our NHS has become a life's ambition for Farage - there's no reason to believe he'll stop now. Whether it be in England or in Nye Bevan's Wales, we can't afford to let Reform get within an inch of our NHS. Labour is fixing our NHS and will always ensure it remains free at the point of use."

Article continues below

Mr Farage had been asked about Reform’s NHS promises during Reform’s Welsh manifesto launch last week, and whether he would consider a French style insurance healthcare system. He said: “That would be a national decision ahead of a general election. All I will say on the national side of it, is there are one or two health trusts in England where they've managed, without huge increases in budgets, to massively increase delivery, and our chairman, David Bull, is, of course, a former GP, and we're looking at best practice.

“As I've said before, on the big UK picture of health, I'm prepared to consider any alternative to the failure we've got now, and the failure is, we've doubled expenditure, and there are no more beds in the NHS over the course of the last 10 years. It's not working.”

Reform said in its 2024 general election manifesto that NHS services “will always be free at the point of use,” though not for foreign citizens. Mr Farage has repeatedly called for a new healthcare system where “If you can afford it, you pay; if you can’t, you don’t”.

News.title: Woman writes flirty note to flight attendant on napkin and something amazing happens
News.content:
Woman writes flirty note to flight attendant on napkin and something amazing happens

A woman shared how she wrote a flirty note to a flight attendant, and then shared the aftermath of her bold move, saying that she can't believe what happened...

View Image The woman was nervous after she penned the flirty note (Stock Image) (Image: dima_sidelnikov via Getty Images)

A woman shared a moment she took the bull by the horns and wrote a flirty note to a flight attendant she thought was cute.

‌



There's nothing quite like going after what you want, and if you get rejected, who cares? Nobody else needs to know, and at least you decided to shoot your shot and attempt to get the attention of the person you thought was cute, rather than mindlessly swiping on dating apps all day long until your thumb feels sore.

‌



That's exactly what one woman did when she thought her "flight attendant was hot," and something incredible happened. She shared her meet-cute on Instagram, where it garnered more than one million likes and almost 11,000 comments.

‌



On the plane, Alycia Viola "rang the bell and asked for a [glass of] champagne," which the dreamy flight attendant handed over to her, full of professionalism. However, she then handed him a note, making sure to mouth that he needed to open it so he didn't just think the napkin was rubbish and throw it away, because that would've scuppered her plans.

Alycia joked that she needed "liquid courage" to ask the flight attendant a question, which was 'Will you get drinks with me after the flight?', with yes written on one side of the napkin, and no on the other.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



Bracing herself, she sent him away with the napkin, and then he subtly returned it just minutes later.

Lo and behold, when he gave it back to her, he'd circled yes, and so the pair ended up going for a drink later that evening, and she wrote over the video "success".

"I can’t believe it’s been two years since I posted this viral moment with my now fiancé," she wrote in the caption, proving that sometimes it pays not to be shy.

‌



In the comments, others shared their meet-cute experiences, with one writing: "Was on a flight to Bali for a weekend with my friends, and the guy sat next to me chatted me up.

"He called and asked me out for dinner the next night, then a week later called me when I was back in Jakarta and asked if I would like to meet him again. That was in 2008. We’re now 18 years together and married for 14 years."

Another shared her aunt's story, penning: "Awww this warms my heart. My aunt met her husband that way, too.

Article continues below

"She used to fly for Emirates, and he was on a business flight. They started talking, and she was playing hard to get. So he gave her his number, hoping she would call or text. Unfortunately, she lost it during her layover.

"But he kept flying Emirates, hoping to bump into her again - AND HE DID! He was a bit bummed at first because he thought she wasn't interested in him, even though they were really hitting it off on the flight they met.

"They exchanged numbers again, this time both ways to be safe. Fast forward to today: they have been married since 2008 and have two beautiful children."

News.title: Russia claims Donald Trump could unleash 'zombie apocalypse' like The Last of Us
News.content:
Russia claims Donald Trump could unleash 'zombie apocalypse' like The Last of Us

The deputy chairman of Russia's Security Council has said the US could unleash a "zombie apocalypse".

Dmitry Medvedev, a former Russian president, claimed American biological research will trigger deadly outbreaks similar to those portrayed in the hit TV series 'The Last of Us'.

In an essay about threats posed by new technology, Medvedev suggested that advances in biotechnology and US military research could lead to catastrophic epidemics. He neglected to mention Vladimir Putin's own use of nerve agents and poisons against his enemies, including Kremlin critic Alexei Navalny.

It comes after scientists at Porton Down, the UK's top secret defence lab in Wiltshire, "conclusively" recently identified epibatidine - a powerful neurotoxin found naturally in South American dart frogs - in smuggled biological samples linked to Navalny, who was found dead in his cell at a remote Arctic penal colony on February 16, 2024, aged 47.

Image: Getty Images) Getty Images)

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

In a lengthy article titled "New Technologies: A Stairway to Heaven or a Road to Hell", Medvedev said: "After some time, the zombie apocalypse demonstrated by Americans themselves in the game and TV series The Last of Us may not be so fantasy."

He claimed the US has created a vast network of around 400 biological laboratories around the world, accusing Washington of conducting research capable of spreading dangerous diseases - allegations Western governments have repeatedly dismissed as disinformation.

"The United States, as is well known, has formed a global military biological network, using other countries as testing grounds," he wrote.

"As a result, millions of people are unwittingly becoming hostages to deadly threats. The infrastructure, created with the direct participation of the Pentagon and its affiliated medical companies, comprises approximately 400 dual-use BSL-class laboratories operating in the Middle East, Southeast Asia, Africa, and the [former Soviet Union].

"The research results allow the US to manage the spread of epidemics and study the genetic factors that influence human resistance to various diseases."

He argued that advances in genetic engineering and synthetic biology could allow new pathogens to be created artificially and spread in ways that would be difficult to trace.

Image: Getty) Getty)

He warned that AI could destroy millions of jobs, unleash cyberattacks on critical infrastructure and even take control of vital systems such as nuclear power plants.

Medvedev also claimed that swarms of drones could transform warfare into a video-game-like "remote killing" system, lowering the psychological barrier to violence. "The existence of all humanity, whose situation is becoming increasingly precarious and bleak, is at stake," he said.

The hardline Kremlin figure accused Western tech giants and governments of using digital platforms to "softly enslave" populations through algorithms and data control.

Medvedev, who served as Russia’s president from 2008 to 2012, has become one of the Kremlin’s most prominent anti-Western voices since Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022.

He frequently posts inflammatory statements online predicting the collapse of the West, nuclear war or the destruction of Ukraine.

In his latest broadside, he also claimed the war in Ukraine - which he described as a crisis "fuelled by Russophobic countries seeking Russia’s defeat" - would eventually end as global attention shifted to technological threats facing humanity.

News.title: 'Keep house tidy' with Dunelm's 'chic' storage unit now at lowest ever price
News.content:
'Keep house tidy' with Dunelm's 'chic' storage unit now at lowest ever price

The clearance sale slashes the cost down to almost half its original price

View 3 Images The clearance event slashes the cost of this item to half of its original price (Image: Peter Dazeley/Getty Images)

People have been raving about a "pretty and practical" piece of furniture that helps to "spruce up an entryway" and avoid clutter. As part of Dunelm's most recent clearance sale, the item has been reduced to its lowest price ever .

The Bamboo Shoe Rack with Table is normally £40, but during Dunelm's latest round of sales, it has been slashed to just £20 for either style. Shoppers can pick from a more natural wood effect or a solid white finish.

Bosses from the homeware store said: " Keep your hallway calm and collected with our Bamboo Shoe Rack with Table, a clever blend of style and storage. With its chic, minimalist design, it's a natural fit for modern homes.





"The top table adds an extra perch for post, keys or a lovely little houseplant to spruce up your entryway. Its space-saving shape means even compact corners can feel tidy and well-dressed."

Dunelm claims that the table, now £20 instead of £40, offers "fuss-free functionality with a stylish twist". It is not the only kind of bargain side table that shoppers can claim, as other brands have similar products.

View 3 Images The Bamboo Shoe Rack is now half price (Image: Dunelm)

Debenhams is selling a Five-Tier Foldable Bamboo Shoe Rack (down from £50 to £24.01), and Matalan has the HOMCOM White Three-Tier Shoe Rack on offer for £24. Amazon stocks the Vencier Bamboo Shoe Rack with options ranging from two to five tiers (from £16.99 to £29.99), but the Dunelm option has rave reviews to back its major price drop.

One shopper said: " Very useful piece of furniture. Pretty and practical. Would most definitely recommend." Another wrote: "Looks clean and simple. A practical height for putting small items down in the hallway. Very narrow, so it doesn't take any more space than necessary."

Someone else put: " I like the table bit on top so you can display a few bits and pieces. It is a little bit small for my husband's size 11 shoes, but they squeeze on. It looks good and fits nicely into our hallway."

View 3 Images One shopper said: "Very useful piece of furniture" (Image: Dunelm)

Overall, from the reviews, Dunelm awards the Bamboo Shoe Rack 4.4 stars out of a possible five. This includes 29 reviews with just a handful of critics, with one of them claiming their bamboo version came with a top part that in "a colour that doesn't match", suggesting it was "a bit greenish-orangeish and overall looks off".

Someone else suggested in a three-star review: "Table/shoe rack is the perfect size for a gap in my hallway and was easy to put together. I would say it’s not very robust, so maybe it wouldn’t stand up to anything heavy put on it."

Article continues below

And, one more warned shoppers: "The only reason for not giving five stars is that the tray on the top is not attached. It just sits on top of the frame, which is a bit annoying if you need to move the rack for cleaning the floor."

But, the majority of the reviews offered this bargain piece of furniture a five-star award. One of those satisfied shoppers put in their review: "Absolutely love it, it is so strong and sturdy, and with the shelf I can put all my lovely things on there. It definitely takes the look of just shoes away with the eye-catching decor displayed."

News.title: Donald Trump slammed as 'ridiculous lies' about Iran war are brutally exposed
News.content:
Donald Trump slammed as 'ridiculous lies' about Iran war are brutally exposed

President Donald Trump has been slammed for his handling of the ongoing Iran conflict. It's been claimed his "ridiculous lies" about the war have finally been exposed

View Image President Trump has been slammed for his "ridiculous lies" (stock image) (Image: Getty Images)

Turmoil in the Middle East continues unabated and President Donald Trump has once again been criticised for his management of the war. Nearly two weeks have passed since President Trump confirmed the US military's collaboration with Israel in an attempt to topple Iran's current regime, yet he seems to be delivering conflicting messages about the situation.

‌



He's recently been called out after speaking to Fox News about the unfolding events in Iran, and he's now been slammed for telling what's been described as a "ridiculous lie". This comes as people globally express concern about how escalating tensions might affect other nations.

‌



It's not the only time he's faced opposition recently either. Previously he also made a claim about the UK that was deemed to be bonkers.

‌



Hal, known online as Hal for NY, has now urged Trump to "get his lies" straight" after he made perplexing comments during a recent interview with Fox News. Detailing what transpired, Hal stated: "Fox News just called out Donald Trump right to his face over his hypocrisy and double speak when talking about his war in Iran.

"Now this was in Ohio where Donald Trump was touring a factory, during which he started going on about the Iran war, and he called it a 'little excursion' and then he called it a 'war'.

"Well, Fox News apparently had enough of this double speak, and they called him out. Take a listen."

‌



Hal then shows a snippet of the interview where Trump is asked to clarify whether the attack is an excursion or a war, to which Trump responds: "Well, it's both."

Hal stated you "know it's getting bad" when even Fox News call him out; however, a crucial question was posed. Hal pointed out that Trump went on to describe it as a "little excursion" to halt the war, suggesting it qualifies as both.

He declared this is "ridiculous" to state. Hal continued: "It's getting to the point where it's beyond ridiculous that he cannot get the story straight, and why we are going to Iran, and what the goals are, especially when you consider we have already lost seven service members, and there are over 150 that have already been injured.

‌



"The President of the United States, the commander in chief, needs to get his story straight, and good on Fox News to join the party."

Content cannot be displayed without consent

The video has since garnered a lot of attention, with dozens commenting to share their views. It's clear many viewed his response as utterly outrageous.

‌



Some did highlight they believe he intended to say "incursion", which is a sudden, often hostile or unwanted, intrusion into a territory. It sparked a lot of conversation.

One individual commented: "Anything Trump says is ridiculous. Period." Another explained precisely what an excursion is, adding: "An excursion is a short, organised pleasure trip or outing, often taken by a group to a specific location for sightseeing, education or leisure.

"These trips typically last a few hours to a full day, offering benefits like relaxation, cultural immersion and adventure. Common examples include guided tours, city visits, nature hikes and beach trips."

Article continues below

A third responded: "Little excursions don't cost hundreds of lives." Meanwhile, a fourth remarked: "I think he's getting incursion and excursion mixed up."

Another person also added: "It's not surprising. He's never got a story straight in his life. Every time he tells the same story, it's different. If that one doesn't work, then he'll tell you another one."

Earlier this week, Trump suggested the war may be over "soon", though attacks have still been launched this week. There is no definitive timeline as to when tensions might subside in the Middle East.

News.title: Lloyds, Bank of Scotland and Halifax apps showed customers other users' transactions
News.content: "I can see another person's bank account, he got paid £6,000 yesterday. Others, I can see their benefits payments, their National Insurance numbers, I can see where they work, almost their whole identity."

News.title: Hundreds of GPs tell BBC they have never refused a fit note for mental health concerns
News.content: But many pointed out that it was especially hard to refuse time off for mental health complaints. One asked: "Who am I to say I don't believe that someone is struggling with their mental health? It's not like a chest infection where there is definite proof of illness."

News.title: Minister defends PM's handling of Mandelson appointment
News.content: He said Sir Keir has not sought to move on from the issue but instead is "understanding the damage that it has done and then putting in place changes to public life, to the very structures of power in our society … that have ignored what has happened to women and girls at the hands of powerful men".

News.title: My phone contents were shared with the police colleague I accused of rape
News.content:
Family's fight for answers after prison death 'felt like torture'

Allan Marshall was on remand at HMP Edinburgh in 2015 when he was restrained face down by up to 17 prison officers.

News.title: Draper fights to 'mind-blowing' win over Djokovic
News.content:
Britain's Jack Draper produced a superb fightback to beat Novak Djokovic and reach the Indian Wells fourth round - a result he believes could be "a real big moment" in his season.

Defending champion Draper regrouped impressively to beat 24-time Grand Slam champion Djokovic 4-6 6-4 7-6 (7-5) in a "real physical battle" of two hours and 35 minutes.

Djokovic, a five-time winner at Indian Wells, had beaten Draper in the only previous meeting between them at Wimbledon in 2021.

The victory is an important one for Draper as he rebuilds after a torrid eight-month spell plagued with an arm injury.

"To beat Novak is something that is mind-blowing," Draper, who is playing only his second ATP Tour event of the season, told BBC Sport.

"I've watched him since I was a kid, idolising him. Unbelievable respect for the player that he is.

"I still want to play better, and feel better on the court, but I think for how long I have had off the court to come here and play three tough matches already and play that one, it's huge for me.

"It's something I will probably look back on in a few months and think this is a real big moment for me."

World number 14 Draper is joined in the quarter-finals by fellow Briton Cameron Norrie, who will play world number one Carlos Alcaraz next.

News.title: Epstein used modelling agent to recruit girls, Brazilian women tell BBC
News.content: Describing the trip to France, she says: "He [Epstein] would give me $300 (£225). I would go out for a walk and give him the change, but he would tell me to keep the money. He would test me and leave money in my room, and then I would give it back to him, and he would say I could keep it."

News.title: Student loans inquiry to look at whether system is 'unfair to graduates'
News.content: The committee is inviting anyone over the age of 16 to share their experiences of the system via an online survey. It will look at the system in England but will welcome submissions from other parts of the UK.

News.title: 'It was the stuff of dreams' - Liza Tarbuck leaves Radio 2 Saturday show
News.content: "Every Saturday evening, Liza created a truly magical, fantastical world," Thomas said. "She was beloved by everyone at the station and all her listeners, and the door at Radio 2 will always be open for her."

Boxing.title: Donald Trump gives Jake Paul shock endorsement for political career as boxer joins President at Kentucky rally
Boxing.content:
President Donald Trump brought boxing star Jake Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday and made a bold prediction about the social media star's future.

The 29-year-old Ohio native, who has transitioned from a YouTube personality to a major force in the combat sports world, stood alongside the president as the crowd in Hebron, Ky roared in support.

During the event, Trump signaled that Paul's influence could eventually shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government in the coming years.

'I'm going to make a prediction that you will be, in the not too distant future, running for political office,' Trump told the fighter and the assembled supporters.

The president didn't stop at a mere prediction, immediately offering his political backing to the 'Problem Child' should he decide to pursue a seat in the future.

'And you have my complete and total endorsement,' the president added, cementing a public alliance with one of the most polarizing and popular figures in modern media.

President Donald Trump brought boxing star Jake Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday

Trump suggested that Paul could shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government

Paul expressed his admiration for the president and credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience

Addressing the Kentucky crowd, Paul expressed his admiration for the president and credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience.

He told the crowd that the president had taught him the value of 'courage' and to 'never back down from a fight,' a mantra Paul has lived by throughout his boxing career.

'We need more factories like this thriving all over the country,' Paul said, emphasizing his support for the president's economic vision for the American Heartland.

'And I know [Trump's] going to be the one to bring that here to us,' he continued, signaling his belief in a resurgence of American manufacturing.

The fighter also leaned into a spiritual message, telling the audience: 'I know God is with us. I know he wants us on the right side of history, and everyone here has to do their part.'

'And God's got us. Trump's got us. God bless,' Paul concluded, before reflecting on the local spirit of the attendees in the Bluegrass State.

'I feel all the local Kentuckians feel the same way -- you guys have that fight, you guys have that swag. There's a lot of young kids in here. The future of America.'

Trump returned the praise, telling the crowd: 'He's a great guy. He's a courageous guy and a talented guy. He's a hell of a fighter, too, by the way.'

'We need more factories like this thriving all over the country,' Paul said, emphasizing his support for the president's economic vision

Paul's most recent endeavor in the boxing ring saw him take on British star Anthony Joshua

The endorsement of Paul's fighting spirit comes as the 29-year-old continues to dominate headlines following his endeavors in the boxing ring over recent years.

Paul has transformed the boxing landscape by leveraging his massive digital following to create events that have outdrawn traditional championship bouts in viewership and revenue.

His most recent endeavor in the ring saw him take on British heavyweight star Anthony Joshua, which ended in a knockout victory for 'AJ'.

Whether Paul follows Trump's advice and swaps the gloves for a suit remains to be seen.

Boxing.title: Anthony Joshua quits the UK to move to Dubai - even as thousands try to flee amid rising panic in Middle East warzone
Boxing.content:
Anthony Joshua quits the UK to move to Dubai - even as thousands try to flee amid rising panic in Middle East warzone

Boxing superstar Anthony Joshua is quitting the UK to move to Dubai - even though the desert city is now in a warzone with thousands of Britons desperate to get out.

The former heavyweight world champion, 36, has formally confirmed he will now be resident in the United Arab Emirates despite it currently coming under nightly attack from Iranian suicide drones.

Joshua, who was born in Watford of Nigerian heritage, won a gold medal at the 2012 London Olympics and has since been awarded both OBE and MBE honours - but now is quitting his home country.

The surprise move by 'AJ' - as he is known to fans - is revealed in the small print of paperwork he filed on Friday for his £150million business empire in which he is legally required to state the country in which he lives.

And, extraordinarily, the documentation shows that Joshua has pressed ahead with his decision to change his residential status despite the war between the USA and Israel and Iran which has seen neighbouring UAE come under sustained attack.

Anthony Joshua is moving to Dubai - as revealed in the small print of his paperwork

Footage taken from social media shows Saturday's drone strike on Dubai International Airport

As well as the weather and facilities, Dubai is popular with the wealthy because there is no income tax there.

Joshua would have been liable to pay UK tax on his dividend payment of £10.1million in 2024 and £6.3million in 2023.

His company Sparta Promotions also made profits of £20.396million and paid £6.65million in UK tax in 2024.

Joshua confirmed the decision in the same week that recent UK sport emigre Rio Ferdinand and his wife Kate spoke about what it is like to live in Dubai while it is under missile attack.

Those attacks have seen thousands of Britons living in or visiting Dubai when the war broke out left desperate to get out - with some paying as much as £100,000 to secure evacuation on a private jet.

Filings for two of Joshua's companies, Sparta Promotions Limited and 258 Investments Limited, both show the formal change in where he will from now on reside.

Joshua has long been a regular visitor to Dubai where he uses the renowned sporting facilities for holidays and training, as well as promotional work for brands he represents.

The most spectacular stunt in this vein came in 2017 when he undertook a training sparring session on the helipad of the famous sail-shaped Burj Al Arab hotel - a 90-foot wide landing platform suspended 700 feet above the sea.

Passengers in the terminal at Dubai Airport on Saturday after the airport closed again due to drone strikes

He visited the city late last year, dining out with boxing promoter Eddie Hearn, and again last month when he had training sessions with MMA fighter Khabib Nurmagomedov.

The striking decision to shift base to Dubai follows the tragic road accident in Nigeria last year in which claimed the lives of two close friends, personal trainer Kevin 'Latz' Ayodele and long-time therapist Sina Ghami.

Joshua has been in recovery ever since the ordeal and recently posted pictures of rehab work on his ribs via Snapchat.

He captioned the post: 'Rehabilitation protocol. When you go thru certain things, you realise that you are stronger than you think you are.'

The move makes Joshua the latest high-profile wealthy Briton to be based overseas following the likes of Manchester United owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe and EasyJet founder Stelios Haji-Ioannou, who both live in Monaco, and Pimlico Plumbers tycoon Charlie Mullins, who is in Marbella.

The rules on tax for those with ex-pat status are complex.

If individuals spend 183 days or more in the UK they are considered a UK tax resident, but if it is fewer than 16 days they would not be considered resident and eligible for taxes.

For in-between cases, HMRC officials have a complex 'Statutory Residence Test' to determine whether UK tax has to be paid, weighing factors like how much time is spent in each place and how established their ties are in both countries.

Joshua is pictured emerging from the car crash that claimed his friends' lives

Joshua pictured with friends Latif Ayodele (centre) and Sina Ghami, who both died in the crash

So to maximise any tax saving Joshua could be forced to spend barely two weeks a year here.

In December it was revealed that 'AJ' had spent £1.2 million to secure a 15,300 sq ft penthouse, the Sky Palace, in the new Park Lane 101 development in Sultan Haitham City, in Oman, which is 300 miles away.

But it is thought the apartment is an investment property and it is not clear if he has yet settled on a permanent base in Dubai.



There has been speculation that the attacks on Dubai could see property prices there fall by as much as 20%.

Boxing.title: Amir Khan heads to Africa again as boxing promoters battle for control of the sport
Boxing.content:
Promoter Amir Khan is staging another boxing card in Africa, positioning himself in a rapidly shifting promotional landscape where global power brokers are increasingly battling for control of the sport.
